Cost Pricing

Gutter Cleaning Cost in the Shuswap: Pricing, What's Included, and Why It Matters

Published 2026-05-11 · Wagon Windows

Gutters are one of those maintenance items that feel optional until they fail — and when they fail, the bill is not a gutter cleaning bill. It's a fascia replacement bill, a foundation repair bill, or a basement remediation bill. Regular gutter cleaning in the Shuswap is cheap prevention against expensive consequences.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Get an Instant Estimate (403) 837-5092

Shuswap Gutter Cleaning Prices: What Wagon Windows Charges

Wagon Windows prices gutter cleaning at $149 for single-storey homes and $169 for two-storey homes in the Shuswap. These are flat rates that apply across the service area — Salmon Arm, Blind Bay, Sorrento, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, Armstrong, and surrounding communities. What's included in the flat rate: all gutters cleared of debris (leaves, cottonwood seed pods, conifer needles, organic matter), downspout flushing to clear blockages and confirm flow, and a basic condition report noting any visible damage to gutters, fascia, or downspout connections. The condition report isn't a formal inspection, but it's a professional eye on the state of your drainage system — useful information about problems that need attention before water damage sets in. What's not included: gutter repairs, gutter guard installation, or remediation of existing water damage. If cleaning reveals a section of gutter pulling away from fascia or a cracked downspout elbow, that's flagged in the condition report and is a separate repair. Knowing about those issues early is the point — catching a loose gutter bracket before it fails is a $40 hardware store fix; missing it until the gutter pulls away and directs water down the siding is an insurance claim.

  • Single-storey gutter cleaning: $149
  • Two-storey gutter cleaning: $169
  • Included: debris removal, downspout flush, condition report
  • Not included: repairs, gutter guard installation, water damage remediation

Why Shuswap Gutters Need Cleaning Twice a Year

Two cleanings per year is the right cadence for most Shuswap properties, and the local ecosystem makes a strong case for it. Spring, specifically late April through June, is cottonwood season. Cottonwood trees are among the most prolific seed-shedders in the Shuswap and across Interior BC generally. The white seed fluff they produce doesn't just drift across your windshield — it packs into gutters, compresses when wet, and creates dense mats that block water flow within weeks of the trees releasing. A spring clean in late May or early June, after cottonwood season peaks, clears these mats before summer rain events. Fall, September through October, is the deciduous leaf-drop. Birch, maple, and deciduous trees common in Salmon Arm's established neighbourhoods deposit large volumes of leaf material. Wet leaves pack solid in gutters, retain moisture through winter, and create ideal conditions for wood rot in the underlying fascia. A fall clean before the first hard freeze protects the drainage system through the winter period when it's inaccessible. Conifers — Ponderosa pine and Douglas fir are abundant across the Shuswap — shed needles year-round but particularly in late summer. Properties surrounded by conifers may benefit from a mid-summer check, though for most homes the twice-yearly schedule catches the significant accumulations.

  • Spring (May–June): cottonwood fluff packs gutters solid
  • Fall (Sept–Oct): deciduous leaf drop before freeze
  • Conifers: year-round needle shed, peaks in late summer

What Happens When Shuswap Gutters Don't Get Cleaned

The consequences of blocked gutters are well-documented and genuinely expensive. Understanding them makes the $149–$169 annual or biannual cost feel different. Overflowing gutters don't just look bad — they dump water at the foundation. Salmon Arm gets sufficient summer rainfall and spring snowmelt that a single blocked-gutter overflow event during a heavy rain can direct hundreds of litres of water against the foundation. Over years, this causes foundation cracking and basement moisture intrusion. In older Shuswap homes — many built in the 1960s through 1980s with foundations that weren't designed for modern drainage expectations — this risk is meaningful. Water overflowing behind the gutter soaks the fascia board. Fascia rot typically develops slowly and invisibly until it's extensive. By the time it's visible, replacement is often required on large sections. Fascia replacement on a two-storey Shuswap home runs $800–$3,000+ depending on extent and access. A $149 annual gutter clean is objectively better use of the money. Blocked downspouts add another failure mode: water that can't exit through the downspout backs up in the gutter and eventually overflows in the gutter section above the blockage, often at an uncontrolled point away from any drainage control. In Shuswap winters, blocked gutters fill with standing water that freezes. Ice dams add weight that pulls gutters from fascia and can force water under shingles at the eave. Cleaning gutters in the fall prevents the conditions that create spring ice dam damage.

Combining Gutter Cleaning with Window Cleaning for a Better Deal

Many Shuswap homeowners book gutter cleaning and window cleaning together in the spring and again in the fall. There's a practical efficiency to this: a cleaning crew is already at the home, equipment is set up, and the combined booking reduces total scheduling overhead. Wagon Windows offers both services and can combine them in a single visit. The spring combination — post-cottonwood window cleaning plus gutter clearing after the seed fluff has settled — is the most popular schedule for the Salmon Arm area. It takes care of the home's exterior maintenance in a single booking before hosting season. The fall combination (window clean before the mud and leaf-fall season, gutter clean after peak leaf-drop) works similarly well, though timing is slightly trickier: you want to clean gutters after most leaves have fallen but before the first hard freeze, which gives a window of roughly late September through mid-October in the Salmon Arm area. For properties in Blind Bay, Sorrento, and the Shuswap Lake shoreline areas, the spring combination is particularly important because lake-facing windows accumulate mineral spray through the ice-off period, and gutters on lake-adjacent properties tend to accumulate algae and organic debris from the moist lakeside environment.

Gutter Cleaning for Shuswap Properties with Complicated Roof Lines

Not all gutters are equally accessible. Standard single-storey gutters are serviced quickly from a 6-8 foot ladder. Two-storey gutters, gutters above attached garages, and gutters on homes with steep roof pitches require more equipment and time. The Shuswap has a high proportion of properties with complex roof lines — steep-pitched A-frames, chalet-style lake properties, homes with wrap-around verandas and continuous gutters at multiple levels. These properties still require two cleans per year; the equipment just needs to match the access. Wagon Windows services these properties as part of the standard two-storey rate where applicable, or by custom quote for configurations outside the standard bracket. When booking, describe your home's storeys, any attached structures with separate gutters (garages, covered decks), and any unusual roof pitch that you're aware of. Homes on hillside lots in Salmon Arm present a version of the two-storey problem: the rear of the home accesses the roof at a different effective height than the front. Book as a two-storey if your back gutter height from grade exceeds standard single-storey reach.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does gutter cleaning take for a typical Salmon Arm home?

A single-storey home typically takes 45–90 minutes. A two-storey with longer gutter runs, downspout flushing, and debris removal runs 1.5–3 hours. Homes with heavy cottonwood buildup or multiple attached structures take longer.

Is the downspout flush always included?

Yes, for Wagon Windows. Downspout flushing is part of the standard gutter clean because a cleared gutter that drains into a blocked downspout isn't a solved problem. The flush confirms the full drainage path is clear.

Can I combine gutter and window cleaning in one booking?

Yes, and it's a common booking choice for spring and fall maintenance. Call (403) 837-5092 or book at wagonwindow.com and specify both services — the crew brings the right equipment for both.

My gutters overflow during rain but look clean from the ground — what's happening?

A blocked downspout is the most common culprit. Gutters can look empty from below while a packed debris plug in the downspout prevents drainage. The gutter fills and overflows upstream of the blockage. A professional flush will clear it.
